text:
Once upon a time, in a sleepy little town nestled between rolling hills and distant forests, there was a peculiar library. Not just any library. This one had walls lined with thick books that whispered secrets long forgotten. The librarian, Mrs. Jenkins, was known for her strange habits. She wore gloves even in summer and had an owl perched on her elbow.

One wet evening, rain drumming loud and angry against the windows, Strange Things began happening. Books flew off the shelves. Silent screams echoed late at night. Children whispered about ghosts. However, Strange mysteriously got quieter.

That's when Max found it. An old key nestled among moth-eaten bookmarks. Intrigued, Max unlocked something. Lights flickered. Shadows danced. A door creaked open. Into the library crawled… a wise old owl. Nothing scary. Just wise.

"It's just weather wanting in," said the owl. "But keep an eye. Secrets might wanna hide."

 Months later, odd letters
